mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration and weakening consumer spending, which suppresses corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, invalidating the negative earnings channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand growth, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in over 60 percent of triggered instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity constraints and rising input costs, which compress profit margins and trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases, or the signal predictive power vanishes outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates above 3.0 percent impose high financing costs on leveraged firms and reduce equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0 percent, contradicting the valuation compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor demand, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive returns when PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls, or the interaction effect shows a negative Rank IC over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect strong residential investment and construction activity, which boosts related sector revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 3-year rolling window, or HOUST increases consistently precede negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 105.0 indicates strong manufacturing output and economic momentum, which improves corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of periods where INDPRO crosses above 105.0, invalidating the earnings momentum channel.
